revision.text:
[[ja:法王寺古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Hōōji Kofun |image = [[File:Yosanoumi Special School, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Located at {{ill|Kyoto Prefectural Yosanoumi Support School|ja|京都府立与謝の海支援学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefectural Yosanoumi Support School|1=Kyoto Prefectural Yosanoumi Support School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11375210}} |affiliation = |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Yosa District, Kyoto|en|Yosa District, Kyoto|qid=Q1078639|lt=Yosa District, Kyoto|1=Yosa District, Kyoto}} {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|qid=Q1203259|lt=Yosano|1=Yosano, Kyoto}} Otokoyama
(Located at {{ill|Kyoto Prefectural Yosanoumi Support School|ja|京都府立与謝の海支援学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefectural Yosanoumi Support School|1=Kyoto Prefectural Yosanoumi Support School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11375210}}) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.57435|N|135.1701|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |length = 74m |built = {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} |burial = Unknown |buried_person = |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} coffins, stone pillow, haniwa fragments |historic_site_status = None |notable_features = Mound no longer exists |map = Japan Kyoto |map_dot_label = Hōōji Kofun |map_dot_label = top |type = Zenpokoenfun-SW |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Hōōji Kofun''' (法王寺古墳) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|en|Yosano, Kyoto|qid=Q1203259|lt=Yosano, Kyoto|1=Yosano, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Yosa District, Kyoto|en|Yosa District, Kyoto|qid=Q1078639|lt=Yosa District|1=Yosa District, Kyoto}}, Kyoto Prefecture. The mound was of the {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} type, but it no longer exists. == Overview == The Hōōji Kofun was built at the tip of a hill on the northern shore of {{ill|Aso Bay|ja|阿蘇海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aso Bay|1=Aso Inland Sea|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11657656}}, an inland sea separated by {{ill|Amanohashidate|en|Amanohashidate|qid=Q259727|lt=Amanohashidate|1=Amanohashidate}} in northern Kyoto Prefecture. Though much of the site had been leveled, it was excavated in 1968 by the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education. After the excavation, the site was completely leveled, and the {{ill|Kyoto Prefectural Yosanoumi Support School|ja|京都府立与謝の海支援学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefectural Yosanoumi Support School|1=Kyoto Prefectural Yosanoumi Support School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11375210}} was constructed on its location.{{Sfn|男山村（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|法王寺古墳（古墳）|1989}} The mound was oriented with its {{ill|Zenpo portion|ja|前方部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpo portion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facing southwest. The {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} consisted of a three-tiered circular rear mound and a two-tiered square front mound.{{Sfn|京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック 第3集|2021}} Surrounding the mound were rows of locally distinctive {{ill|Tango-type cylindrical haniwa|ja|丹後型円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango-type cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and layers of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}. Two haniwa coffins were found near the north side of the circular rear mound and at its base, while a wooden coffin was found at a location separate from the tip of the front mound.{{Sfn|京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック 第3集|2021}} The construction period is estimated to be during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, specifically in the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}.{{Sfn|京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック 第3集|2021}} The Hōōji Kofun is the largest kofun in the {{ill|Nodagawa River|ja|野田川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nodagawa River|1=Nodagawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11645972}} basin and the Aso Bay region, predating the later prosperity of the {{ill|Tango Province|en|Tango Province|qid=Q1054331|lt=Tango Province|1=Tango Province}}. The surrounding area, known for ancient prosperity, includes sites such as the {{ill|Ōfuro Minami No.1 Kofun|ja|大風呂南1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfuro Minami No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Yosano and burial goods like the {{ill|Kagami of Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社伝世鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami of Kono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Miyazu.{{Sfn|男山村（平凡社）|1981}} burial goods recovered from the site, such as Sarcophaguss, are displayed at the {{ill|Tango Regional Museum|ja|京都府立丹後郷土資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Regional Museum|1=Kyoto Prefectural Tango Regional Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q104721140}} in Miyazu. == Mound Specifications == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|法王寺古墳（古墳）|1989}} * Total length: 74 meters * Circular rear mound diameter: 55 meters * Square front mound length: 30 meters The kofun Ranks as the seventh largest in the Tango region. The mound was positioned to face Aso Bay. During the excavation, a {{ill|Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of tuff was found at a nearby shrine. Initially, it was thought to be from the Hōōji Kofun. However, later analysis revealed that it dated to a period later than the Hōōji Kofun.{{Sfn|京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック 第3集|2021}} File:法王寺古墳出土 丹後型円筒埴輪 (京都府立丹後郷土資料館展示).JPG|Recovered haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at the Tango Regional Museum.}} File:法王寺古墳出土 丹後型円筒埴輪 (与謝野町古墳公園はにわ資料館展示).JPG|Recovered haniwa
{{small|Exhibited at Yosano Kofun Park Haniwa Museum.}} File:Hououji Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Reference: Nagamochi-style sarcophagus lid
{{small|Exhibited at the Tango Regional Museum.}} == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tango Regional Museum|ja|京都府立丹後郷土資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Regional Museum|1=Kyoto Prefectural Tango Regional Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q104721140}} (Miyazu) – Displays burial goods from the Hōōji Kofun. * {{ill|Yosano Kofun Park Haniwa Museum|ja|与謝野町古墳公園はにわ資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yosano Kofun Park Haniwa Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yosano) – Exhibits haniwa from the Hōōji Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used in writing this article:}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=男山村|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} 26: Kyoto Prefecture|publisher=Heibonsha|isbn=4582490263|ref={{Harvid|男山村（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Sato Koichi|year=1989|chapter=Hōōji Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyodo Shuppan|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|法王寺古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2021|chapter=Hōōji Kofun|title=Kyoto Prefecture Historic Sites and Ruins Handbook Vol. 3|publisher=Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|京都府の史跡・遺跡ハンドブック 第3集|2021}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ōfuro Minami No.1 Kofun|ja|大風呂南1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōfuro Minami No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|法王寺古墳|version=97071439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:法王寺古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q48751107}} [[Category:History of Yosano]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
